reasoning: The Lean statement captures exactly the informal claim. The quantifier "for all n ∈ ℤ⁺" is represented by the implicit binder `{n : ℕ+}`, which ranges over positive integers — equivalent to ℤ⁺. The vector space ℝⁿ is encoded as `Fin n → ℝ`, which is the standard Mathlib model of n-tuples of reals. The phrase "isomorphic as vector spaces over ℚ" is captured by `≃ₗ[ℚ]` (i.e. `LinearEquiv (RingHom.id ℚ) _ _`), using the canonical ℚ-module structures on ℝ and on `Fin n → ℝ`. The existence of an isomorphism is expressed by `Nonempty`, which is standard. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the types match. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
